title: Molecular Basis of Inheritance
subtitle: DNA structure, replication, transcription, genetic code, translation, regulation, genome project and DNA fingerprinting - visually compressed for NEET.

heroModel.title: Central dogma studio
heroModel.subtitle: Drag to rotate - scroll to zoom

roadmapTitle: DNA stores information, copies it, expresses it and can identify individuals.
roadmapDescription: Follow the molecule: DNA structure -> replication -> transcription -> translation -> regulation -> genome tools.
flowchart:
flowchart LR
  A[DNA structure] --> B[Genetic material proof]
  B --> C[Replication]
  C --> D[Transcription]
  D --> E[Genetic code]
  E --> F[Translation]
  F --> G[Gene regulation]
  G --> H[Genome project]
  H --> I[DNA fingerprinting]

roadmap.title: DNA as genetic material
roadmap.summary: Transforming principle, bacteriophage experiments and RNA in some viruses.

roadmap.title: DNA structure
roadmap.summary: Double helix, antiparallel strands, base pairing and nucleosomes.

roadmap.title: Replication
roadmap.summary: Semiconservative copying with polymerase, primers and replication fork.

roadmap.title: Transcription
roadmap.summary: DNA-dependent RNA synthesis with promoter, coding strand and template strand.

roadmap.title: Genetic code
roadmap.summary: Triplet, degenerate, universal, non-overlapping and nearly comma-less.

roadmap.title: Translation
roadmap.summary: Ribosome reads mRNA and links amino acids using tRNA adaptors.

roadmap.title: Gene regulation
roadmap.summary: Lac operon explains inducible gene control in bacteria.

roadmap.title: Genome tools
roadmap.summary: Human Genome Project and DNA fingerprinting use sequence variation.

conceptDescription: Each card converts a dense NCERT paragraph into a visual sequence or decision rule.

sections.title: DNA as genetic material
sections.description: Experiments showed DNA carries hereditary information.
points: Griffith observed transformation in bacteria.
Avery, MacLeod and McCarty identified DNA as transforming principle.
Hershey and Chase used bacteriophages to prove DNA enters bacteria.
RNA is genetic material in some viruses.
sections.neet: Hershey-Chase used radioactive phosphorus for DNA and sulfur for protein.

sections.title: DNA double helix and packaging
sections.description: DNA is a polymer of deoxyribonucleotides arranged as antiparallel double helix.
points: A pairs with T using two hydrogen bonds.
G pairs with C using three hydrogen bonds.
DNA strands are antiparallel.
Histones help package DNA into nucleosomes in eukaryotes.
sections.neet: A-T has two H bonds; G-C has three H bonds.

sections.title: Semiconservative replication
sections.description: Each new DNA molecule receives one old strand and one new strand.
points: Meselson and Stahl proved semiconservative replication.
DNA polymerase adds nucleotides in 5 prime to 3 prime direction.
Leading strand is continuous, lagging strand forms Okazaki fragments.
Primase, helicase, polymerase and ligase coordinate replication.
sections.neet: DNA polymerase cannot initiate synthesis without a primer.

sections.title: Transcription
sections.description: Transcription copies genetic information from DNA into RNA.
points: Template strand is read by RNA polymerase.
Promoter and terminator define transcription unit.
In eukaryotes hnRNA undergoes capping, tailing and splicing.
Exons remain in mature RNA, introns are removed.
sections.neet: Splicing removes introns and joins exons.

sections.title: Genetic code
sections.description: Codons are triplets that specify amino acids or stop signals.
points: AUG is start codon and codes for methionine.
UAA, UAG and UGA are stop codons.
Code is degenerate because many amino acids have more than one codon.
Code is nearly universal.
sections.neet: AUG is both start codon and methionine codon.

sections.title: Translation
sections.description: Translation converts mRNA codon sequence into a polypeptide.
points: tRNA acts as adaptor molecule.
Ribosome has sites for tRNA movement.
Charging of tRNA requires aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase.
Peptide bonds form between amino acids.
sections.neet: tRNA has anticodon loop and amino acid attachment site.

sections.title: Lac operon
sections.description: The lac operon is an inducible system controlled by lactose availability.
points: Repressor blocks operator when inducer is absent.
Lactose/allolactose acts as inducer.
Structural genes help metabolise lactose.
Operator, promoter and regulator gene are key terms.
sections.neet: In lac operon, inducer inactivates repressor.

sections.title: Human genome and DNA fingerprinting
sections.description: Genome analysis and sequence variation are used in identification and research.
points: Human Genome Project aimed to sequence human DNA.
Most human DNA does not code for proteins.
VNTRs/STRs vary among individuals.
DNA fingerprinting compares variable regions.
sections.neet: VNTR variation is central to DNA fingerprinting.

modelDescription: Rotate original DNA, replication fork, transcription and code-flow schematics.
highYield: DNA is transforming principle in Avery experiment.
Hershey-Chase proved DNA enters bacteria using labelled phage.
A pairs T, G pairs C.
DNA polymerase works 5 prime to 3 prime.
Okazaki fragments form on lagging strand.
hnRNA processing includes capping, tailing and splicing.
AUG is start codon and methionine.
UAA, UAG, UGA are stop codons.
Genetic code is triplet and degenerate.

mistakes.title: Coding vs template strand
mistakes.body: RNA sequence resembles coding strand except U replaces T, but RNA is made using template strand.

mistakes.title: Replication direction
mistakes.body: Polymerase adds nucleotides only 5 prime to 3 prime, creating lagging strand fragments.

mistakes.title: Introns vs exons
mistakes.body: Introns are removed; exons stay and express.

quiz.question: Meselson and Stahl proved DNA replication is:
quiz.explain: Each daughter DNA has one old and one new strand.

quiz.question: AUG codes for:
quiz.explain: AUG is start codon and codes methionine.

quiz.question: Which codon is not a stop codon?
DNA polymerase synthesises DNA in:
quiz.explain: New DNA strand elongates 5 prime to 3 prime.

quiz.question: tRNA acts as:
quiz.explain: tRNA links codon to amino acid.

quiz.question: In lac operon, lactose acts as:
quiz.explain: Lactose/allolactose induces operon by inactivating repressor.

quiz.question: Hershey-Chase labelled DNA with:
quiz.explain: DNA contains phosphorus, proteins contain sulfur.

quiz.question: DNA fingerprinting uses variation in:
quiz.explain: Variable tandem repeats differ between individuals.

flashcards.back: Each new DNA has one parental and one newly made strand.
Short lagging-strand DNA pieces joined by ligase.
Variable repeat region used in DNA fingerprinting.

revisionTable: A-T two bonds, G-C three bonds
